title: The One With The Birthing Video

lines.text: Central Perk. Rachel is getting a cup of coffee as Joey and Phoebe enter and sit down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Hey, Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Hey, Happy Valentine's Day!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you, too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, so, uh, how's it going living over at Ross'?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's good. Except he makes us watch the Discovery Channel all day long. Did you know that something really boring happened to someone really ugly in the Middle Ages? (to the waitress handing her a cup of coffee) Oh, thank you. I'll see you guys later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye.
Rachel exits with coffee

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There's one lucky to-go cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sighs) Honey, I wish you would get over her. I hate seeing you like this. Is there anything I can do for you? Do you want to look down my top?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks. But maybe later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Gunther, can I get a scone?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (to Joey) You want anything?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what I want? I want a lot of things! I want to be with the woman I love on Valentine's Day! And I want her to love me back! And I want just one moment of relief from the gut-wrenching pain of knowing that that's never going to happen!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: We have red bagels.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, okay.
Opening Credits
Monica and Chandler's. Phoebe and Ross are sitting in the living room talking.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, how does Mona feel about you and Rachel living together?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm actually on my way to tell her right now. Yeah, she's been away all week visiting her parents, but she'll be cool. I mean, she's been so supportive. She-she even got the baby a tiny T-shirt that says, 'Fossils are my friends.'

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh. Come on, Mona, don't kiss ass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, I'm going to take off.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. Oh! Shoot! Oh shoot! Uh, Rachel wanted to see this tape!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a video of my friend giving birth. Could you just bring it back to your apartment?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. (reading the label) 'Candy and Cookie?'

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Candy's the mother, Cookie's the daughter. The father's also Cookie. Why am I friends with these people?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering with something behind her back) Phoebe, c'mere. I want to show you something in the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Monica, grow up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what's behind your back?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nothing. Just something I want to get Phoebe's opinion on for Valentine's Day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You don't want my opinion?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, I'm your older brother, ask me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, big brother. (holds up two erm...revealing articles of clothing) Which of these do you think would make your little sister look hotter, so your best friend would want to do her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (quietly) The red one.
Joey and...wait...just Joey's. Joey is sitting at the counter eating a pizza.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (knocking and entering) Hey. Look, I know you've been really depressed lately, so I brought someone over to cheer you up. Right outside this door is a real, live, furry playmate.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I'm not sleeping with your friend Jane again.
Phoebe goes into the hall and brings a dog inside!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He-hee!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! A dog! Hi! Who, you got to admit, looks a lot like Jane.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is the happiest dog in the world. I borrowed him from my friend Wendy. Now, you can only keep him until he cheers you up. And he will cheer you up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks so much, Pheebs! (to the dog) We are going to have so much fun, yes we are! (the dog sticks his head between Joey's legs) Oh! Not that kind of fun.
Monica and Chandler's. Chandler enters with a bouquet of roses.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Happy Valentine's!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (from her bedroom) Okay! I'll be right out. I'm slipping into something a little less comfortable, and a little more slutty.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (picking up a video from the table) 'Candy and Cookie'. 'Candy and Cookie?' Monica got me porn?! Girl-on-girl porn?! She really must love me!! (Chandler runs over to the TV, puts the tape in, and sits down to enjoy some "porn")
A woman on TV breaths hard

lines.speaker: Man on TV
lines.text: Yeah, just relax.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you, St. Valentine.
The woman groans, moans, grunts, and screams. Chandler's eyes get huge!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Woah, woah, that's not pretty!

lines.speaker: Man on TV
lines.text: Now, push!

lines.speaker: Woman on TV
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Ooh! That hurts!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Worst porn ever! Worst porn ever! (Chandler starts to press buttons on the remote control, frantically.)

lines.speaker: Woman on TV
lines.text: Ohhh! Make it stop!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am trying!
Joey's. Joey is playing fetch with the dog.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Get the ball, ready? Get the ball, get the ball! (Joey pretends to throw it, but really doesn't, and the dog goes running off.) Well, you're cute, but you're not too smart!
The dog returns with a ball that looks exactly like the same one Joey has.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at the ball the dog brought back) Did I just throw this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I accidentally packed these with my stuff. (looks at the dog and gasps) Who is this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that's, uh, that's Phoebe's friend's dog. I don't know what his real name is, but I call him Mozzarella.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (talking with a higher voice, and puckered lips...kind of like you do to a baby or...well...a puppy...it's hard to explain. Just use your imagination!) Oh, well, you are so cute! I wish I could play with you more, but I've got to go to work! I hope I stop talking like this before my marketing meeting, yes I do. Yes I do. (still talking like that) Bye-bye, Joey. Oh, I seriously can't stop it. (exits)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the dog) C'mere. Hey. C'mere. That's Rachel. She's the one who used to live here. Might as well be honest with you-we love her. But we can't have her. I really miss her. Well, hey, you understand, right? You're a guy. (thinks about it and picks up the dog and looks) Well, you used to be.
Monica and Chandler's. Chandler is sitting on the couch staring at the tape on the coffee table with his eyes huge and his mouth wide open.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from her bedroom) So what do you think? (referring to her outfit)
Chandler glances up at Monica with his mouth still wide open and his eyes still huge

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to herself) I've still got it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pointing at the video) Why did you get me this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's yelling...bleeding...dilating. Oh, the dilating...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is this the video of the baby being born? Sweetie, this is Phoebe's. Why were you even watching it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I thought...maybe...you got me porn for Valentine's Day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, if you thought I was going to get you porn for Valentine's Day... (pulls a video out of a basket) you were right! Apparently, it's about a young girl who moves to the big city, you know, in search of stardom, but ends up having sex with a lot of guys! Yeah, it got four starts! (looks closer) Oh, wait a minute. Those aren't stars. Anyway, you want to take a look?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm not really in a sexy mood right now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, remember the first time we saw Jaws?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mm-hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How long it took to go back in the water?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, we can't let this tape wreck Valentine's Day!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't know. You didn't see it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Child-birth, it's a natural thing! It's beautiful.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, beautiful? Really? Beautiful? You think this is beautiful? (picks up the remote and presses a button, and he immediately makes a face that just looks like he's going to throw up!)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Oh, my God! No wonder my mother hates me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, honey, there's-(puts his hand on her leg)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't touch me!
Central Perk. Ross is sitting on the couch reading a magazine as Mona enters.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! (they hug) So, how was Atlantic City?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I brought you back a present.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha-? Oh, come on. You didn't have to-saltwater taffy?! (Mona laughs) Thanks! This is interesting. You know, most people think this is made with sea water, but it's actually made with, uh, salted fresh water. That's not interesting.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I think it's interesting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I do too! I missed you!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I missed you, too! So, how was your week?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it was good! It was good. Actually, the baby started kicking!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: How exciting!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! I know! It was. Oh... the only sad thing is I wasn't around when it happened for the first time.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'm missing out on all this other stuff, too. So, Joey suggested Rachel move in with me.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (laughing) Yeah right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Joey cracks me up! It's like, 'Yeah, why don't you have your ex-wife move in with you? That wouldn't be awkward at all!' (she laughs again)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (not amused) Huh...uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Huh, could you imagine. I go away for a few days, and come back, and my boyfriend is living with some woman he got pregnant! (Mona laughs...yes...again!)
Ross fake laughs, obviously not finding this funny, and he's starting to panic, so he shoves the whole saltwater taffy he's eating in his mouth

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: So, what'd you tell him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (with his mouth full) Just a second! (he fake laughs, but turns his head and starts to break down)
Commercial Break
Central Perk. Ross and Phoebe are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How could you not tell Mona that Rachel is living with you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, she seemed to think it was such a crazy idea! Um, plus, she, uh, she got me taffy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Taffy, really? I've never had any.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ever?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think my mother was too busy planning her suicide to provide saltwater treats. (Ross hands her one) Thank you! So what, you're just never going to tell her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no, I will! I just want to butter her up, first! You know, I'm going to take her to an amazing Valentine's dinner. Do all this romantic stuff, and then, just when she thinks I'm the best boyfriend in the world, then I'm going to tell her that my pregnant ex-girlfriend is living with me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If I haven't said it before: she's a lucky, lucky lady! So, where are you going to-what the mother of crap is up with this stuff? (Referring to the taffy, which she's been chewing this whole time.) Oh, God. Is it gum, is it food? What's the deal? (she swallows it, finally) Oh, it's nice! May I try a pink one?
Joey's. Joey is laying on his recliner, depressed, and the dog is laying on the footrest.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, between her and me being friends, and her history with Ross, it just isn't going to happen. It would be like you falling in love with a cat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, buddy. How's my favorite dog, huh? How's my favorite dog? (the dog doesn't move) You're subdued. (to Joey) Did you give him a beer?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Will you excuse us, we need to talk for a moment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, sure. Go ahead. (pause) Oh, me, right!
Joey follows Phoebe into the kitchen

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He's miserable! What happened to him?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing. We just talked about stuff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What stuff?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rachel stuff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh...Joey, you bummed him out! This was the happiest dog in the world, and he spends half a day with you, and look at him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He's breathing!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I'm going to take him back to Wendy's.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no, no! He's fine! Look, look, look! (picks up the ball) Here's your ball! Get your ball! Get your ball! (he throws the ball and it bounces right next to the dog) Get your ball! My God, what have I done to you, huh? I broke the dog! Pheebs, I broke the dog!
Ross and...Rachel's...I guess I have to call it that now. Rachel is reading on the couch as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I'm, uh, going to pick up Mona. What have you got going tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I've got big Valentine's plans! I've got my Chinese food on the way, and the rest of your saltwater taffy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, it's interesting! Most people think that's made with seawater, when in fact-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, we actually watched the documentary together.
There is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! My Chinese food! Let me get my cash! (runs to her room to get her money)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (opening the door to see Mona standing there) Mona? What are-hi! What are you doing here? I'm, um, supposed to pick you up!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Change of plans, I made you a special Valentine's dinner! Surprise!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he makes some really weird noise hear that sounds kind of like...)Ayyyayyyy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Oh, hey, Mona!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi! Hi. Hi, Rachel! (to Ross) What's she doing here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I have no idea!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'll be watching TV if anybody needs me. (exits to her room)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Seriously, what is she doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh...lately, she just likes hanging out here.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think she's lonely.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay, but it's Valentine's Day!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Can't we just ask her to go?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. She's way to emotional. And by emotional I mean crazy.
Doorbell rings

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not here! That's just my Chinese food!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh, my God! She has food delivered here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mm-hmm. She's-she's emotional, but, but ballsy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what I'm going to do? I'm going to get in my sweats, and eat this in bed! (exits)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And you thought she was going to be in our way! So, why don't you, uh, open the champagne, and I'll be right back. I've got a surprise for you.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: You got another ex-wife back there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (fakes a laugh) Please start drinking! (exits)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) I'm just going to grab the phone.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh, Rachel! Wait! Hey, I hope you don't take this the wrong way, but, but, um... what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry! Do you need the phone?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No, no. Listen, Ross is too nice to say anything, but this is his apartment, and, and, we gotta have some boundaries, so why don't you go back to your place and give us some privacy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (as Ross enters with a present) But, but, Mona, I live here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (handing Mona the present) Happy Valentine's Day! ...Or something to remember me by.
Monica and Chandler's. Monica and Chandler are sitting on the couch still staring at the screen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How long has it been this time?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 90 seconds.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's better. 90 seconds is a long time not to think about it...except all I did was think about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, it haunts me? Up 'til now, the worst thing I ever saw, was my father doing tequila shots off the naked houseboy. After this, I would gladly make that my screensaver!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, well, we have to get past this! Why don't we get rid of the tape and pretend it never existed?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
Chandler takes the tape and sticks it under the chair cushion

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Now all we've got to do is get rid of this chair.
Chandler crawls over to Monica and they move in to kiss, but they can't do it and back up. They move in to kiss again, and kiss very awkwardly for a second, until someone knocks on the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, thank God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I know!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come in!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi! I'm so sorry to barge in on your Valentine's, but I had to get away from all the yelling. Mona is dumping Ross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, my God.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Poor Ross.
Monica and Chandler both look at each other and run over to the window to watch the action in Ross' apartment

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, great. We have to watch him do yoga in his underwear, but for this he closes the drapes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, you know that birthing tape you wanted to see? It's here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, and we should warn you, before you watch it: don't watch it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? You saw it? Is it scary?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, let's just say it's ironic how footage of someone being born can make you want to kill yourself.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, now, wait. Now I'm all freaked out. Come on, you guys will watch it with me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, but I will leave a sweater that smells like me right next to you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: C'mon, seriously, you guys, you're not going to make me watch this alone!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's right...of course not. Honey, get the tape.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (with his arm all the way under the cushion, moving it around) This reminds me of a very specific part of the tape. (Chandler puts the tape in, and they all sit down) Okay, here we go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (watching the tape) Ooh, my! (Rachel jumps when the woman starts screaming) Woah! Why is that baby torturing that woman?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why have I seen this thing three times?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's...still beautiful.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh! It's horrible!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! I know, I'm so sorry for you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, my God!
Monica and Rachel both cover their eyes

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait, you guys, look!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Did her ass explode?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, the baby's out! Look, look!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, look at those little fingers and toes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And look how happy the mom is now!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh...Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, screw you guys, you don't have to do it!
Central Perk. Ross is getting coffee as Joey comes from...God knows where! Some back area of Central Perk around the corner that we've never seen! Weeeiiirrrddd....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey, man!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what's up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh...Mona just dumped me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, man, I'm sorry. Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, with everything that's been going on lately, I haven't exactly been the perfect boyfriend. You know, I, uh, I didn't tell her I got Rachel pregnant. I gave her a key to my apartment, and then had the locks changed! And then I lied to her about Rachel moving in with me. In a way, I actually judge her for not breaking up with me sooner, you know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Still, that really sucks, man. Especially on Valentine's Day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Wait a minute. What are you doing here? Joey Tribbiani without a date on Valentine's Day? What's going on, huh? Girl trouble?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sort of.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't have to seem so happy about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, sorry. Well, look, maybe I can help you with it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I...I d-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, whatever it is, I am sure it has happened to me. Y'know, actually once-once I got dumped during sex.
Two girls sitting at a table next to them look up in disgust, and Ross and Joey move away

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway, so, uh, so what is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Forget about it. It's no big deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: C'mon! Joey! What is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's nothing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey, it's me. Why can't you tell me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, uh...sit down. (they do) Um... there's this woman that I like. A lot. But, uh...it can't happen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She's not a Tribbiani?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I knew it. So, is she someone from work?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, uh, does she like you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sometimes I think maybe she could. But it doesn't matter, because I can't do anything about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it's complicated. She's with this other guy. For a long time. Someone from work, too. And I could never do that to the guy, because we're really good friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, uh, this guy, she used to go out with, is, uh... is he still in love with her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Um... is he a good guy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, he's the best.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then talk to him! He might be fine with it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, it's worth finding out. I mean, if you really like her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do! So much! I can't stop thinking about her! I can't sleep, I-

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, Joey, you know what? You have to go for it. How often does this happen to you, huh? You owe it to yourself.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (placing a cup in front of Ross) Here's your warm milk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm going to...uh...I'm going to, um, put the bourbon in it at home.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway... seriously, uh...just...just talk to the guy, okay? And tell me how it goes. (walks towards the door until...)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's Rachel.
Ross just stands in the doorway, for like a minute...Ahh! What's going to happen next??!!! I can't make it all the way through the Olympics!!!
Closing Credits
Ross and Rachel's. Rachel is standing in front of the TV holding a video.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (thinking) You're going to have a baby, and you need to be prepared. Now, you're going to make yourself watch the whole thing. Just do it! (Rachel puts the tape in the VCR)

lines.speaker: Woman on TV
lines.text: I came to the big city to become a star! I'll do...anything to make that happen!

lines.speaker: Man on TV
lines.text: Anything?
Music starts playing...yes...you know what kind of music...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (thinking) Hmm...maybe it starts with how she gets pregnant.
End
